lever_summary: Dense f64 ascending sort/argsort via a stable LSD radix keyed by an IEEE total-order bit transform. f64_total_order_key maps f64 bits to a u64 whose unsigned order equals f64::total_cmp (the generic sort path's F64 comparator). sort_along_axis_dense_f64 gathers from as_f64_slice(), radix-orders (O(n)), and writes a dense f64 output (sort) or dense i64 indices (argsort), bypassing the SortKey/Literal machinery. Shared radix_pairs_ascending + for_each_sort_slice helpers; the pass74 i64 path was refactored onto them.

alien_graveyard.primitive: radix sort (non-comparison, O(n)) over IEEE total-order keys + dense SoA gather
alien_graveyard.mapping: float radix via the monotone bit transform (flip-all-but-sign for negatives, flip sign for positives) so byte-wise unsigned order reproduces total_cmp; the f64 analog of the pass74 i64 radix

method: Same-worker-calibrated A/B: baseline = tensor_ops.rs git-stashed to the comparison sort; an unchanged full-i64-reduce calibration bench ran alongside in both runs to normalize cross-worker CPU speed. speedup = (baseline_sort/baseline_calib) / (after_sort/after_calib).
command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'sort_64k_f64|sort_calib_reduce_64k_i64' --save-baseline {fB|fA}

change.lever: Added f64_total_order_key + sort_along_axis_dense_f64; extracted radix_pairs_ascending and for_each_sort_slice (shared with the refactored sort_along_axis_dense_i64). sort_along_axis calls the i64 then f64 dense path for ascending; returns to the generic comparison path otherwise.

isomorphism.ordering: unchanged: the generic F64 comparator is lhs.total_cmp(&rhs); f64_total_order_key reproduces std total_cmp's transform (flip all but sign for negatives) then flips the sign bit so byte-wise unsigned radix order == total_cmp order, including -NaN < -inf < ... < -0 < +0 < ... < +inf < +NaN and distinct NaN payloads (keys derive from to_bits, like total_cmp)
isomorphism.stability: LSD radix is stable and the per-slice gather visits in-slice indices ascending, so equal keys keep original order, matching the stable sort_by
isomorphism.direction: ascending only; descending uses the comparison sort
isomorphism.outputs: sort emits a dense f64 output; argsort emits dense i64 in-slice indices — the same fields the generic path writes
isomorphism.tie_breaking: stable, identical to the generic stable sort
isomorphism.errors: returns None (generic path) for non-dense / non-F64 / short axes / non-dividing shapes; no new error classes

validation.name: radix_f64_sort_parity
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --lib sort
validation.result: passed: 13 tests incl radix_sort_f64_ascending_matches_comparison_sort (n=1000 with NaN / payload-NaN / +-inf / +-0 / dups; dense vs Literal/generic sort+argsort compared by bits) and the pass74 i64 cases

validation.name: clippy_deny_warnings
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings
